name: making-commits
capabilities: Guides developers on making small and focused commits
Teaches how to break big changes into logical commits
Encourages running linter and formatting before committing
author: Crossbill-Highlights
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only commit guidelines in SKILL.md. No executable code, file system access, network operations, or cryptographic operations exist. All 16 static findings are false positives caused by the scanner misidentifying JSON string values and metadata fields as security-relevant patterns.
valueStatement: This skill helps developers write better commit messages and structure commits effectively. It provides guidelines for creating a clean, searchable commit history that teams can understand and navigate.
userTitle: Create clear and maintainable git commits
limitations: Does not execute git commands automatically
Does not integrate with git hosting platforms

useCases.title: Improve personal commit habits
useCases.description: Learn to write clear commit messages that help you track changes over time

useCases.title: Establish team commit standards
useCases.description: Guide team members to create consistent and searchable commit histories

useCases.title: Follow project guidelines
useCases.description: Contribute clean commits that project maintainers can review easily

promptTemplates.title: Basic commit help
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need guidance on making commits
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a good commit for this change: [describe your change]

promptTemplates.title: Review commit message
promptTemplates.scenario: When you want to improve a commit message
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my commit message and suggest improvements: [paste your commit message]

promptTemplates.title: Split large changes
promptTemplates.scenario: When you have a big change to commit
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me break down this large change into logical commits: [describe the change]

promptTemplates.title: Team standards
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a commit message guide for my team working on [project type]

bestPractices: Make commits small and focused on one concern
Run linter and formatter before every commit
Write descriptive commit messages that explain why the change was needed
antiPatterns: Creating huge commits with unrelated changes
Committing without running code quality tools
Using vague messages like 'fix' or 'update' without details

faq.question: What platforms support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for git commit guidance.

faq.question: What is the recommended commit size?
faq.answer: Keep commits focused on a single concern. Small commits are easier to review and revert.

faq.question: How does this integrate with my workflow?
faq.answer: Ask for commit guidance when preparing changes. The skill provides recommendations for messages and structure.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only provides guidance. It does not access files or execute git commands.

faq.question: Why should I run linter before committing?
faq.answer: Running linter and formatter ensures consistent code style and catches issues before they enter the commit history.

faq.question: How is this different from commit message tools?
faq.answer: This skill provides guidelines and advice rather than generating commit messages automatically.
